title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure poses a significant threat to organizations relying on cloud services. By exploiting compromised CI/CD pipelines, attackers can deploy malicious code, leading to potential data encryption, service disruptions, and financial losses.
recommended_actions: Implement strict access controls and monitor CI/CD pipelines for unauthorized changes or suspicious activity.
Regularly back up critical data and ensure backups are stored in isolated environments to prevent ransomware encryption.
Deploy endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions and conduct regular security audits of cloud infrastructure.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself does not pose a direct threat but could be exploited by malicious actors if improperly configured or if it contains undisclosed vulnerabilities. Additionally, widespread adoption of the tool without proper vetting could lead to false positives or missed vulnerabilities.
recommended_actions: Conduct a thorough security review of the tool before integrating it into your environment.
Monitor the tool's updates and community feedback to ensure it remains secure and effective.
Implement the tool in a controlled environment and complement it with additional security measures.

title: A vulnerability was detected in Totolink A3300R 17.0.0cu.557_b20221024. The affected element is...
description: A vulnerability was detected in Totolink A3300R 17.0.0cu.557_b20221024. The affected element is the function setVpnPassCfg of the file /cgi-bin/cstecgi.cgi of the component Parameter Handler. Performing a manipulation of the argument pptpPassThru results in command injection. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely. The exploit is now public and may be used.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-5105.

Executing a manipulation of the argument sname can lead to cross site scripting. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. The exploit has been published and may be used.

title: A vulnerability has been found in FRRouting FRR up to 10.5.1. This affects the function...
description: A vulnerability has been found in FRRouting FRR up to 10.5.1. This affects the function process_type2_route of the file bgpd/bgp_evpn.c of the component EVPN Type-2 Route Handler. The manipulation leads to improper access controls. The attack can be initiated remotely. The attack is considered to have high complexity. The exploitability is reported as difficult. The identifier of the patch is 7676cad65114aa23adde583d91d9d29e2debd045. To fix this issue, it is recommended to deploy a patch.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-5107.

title: The Download Monitor plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Insecure Direct Object Reference in...
description: The Download Monitor plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Insecure Direct Object Reference in all versions up to, and including, 5.1.7 via the executePayment() function due to missing validation on a user controlled key. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to complete arbitrary pending orders by exploiting a mismatch between the PayPal transaction token and the local order, allowing theft of paid digital goods by paying a minimal amount for a low-cost item and using that paymen

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Download Monitor plugin allows unauthenticated attackers to exploit payment mismatches, enabling theft of paid digital goods. This can lead to financial losses, unauthorized access to premium content, and potential reputational damage for affected organizations.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the Download Monitor plugin to the latest patched version (if available) or disable the plugin temporarily until a fix is released.
Implement additional server-side validation for payment tokens to ensure they match the local order data before processing transactions.
Monitor payment logs and transactions for suspicious activity, such as mismatched payment amounts or unauthorized access attempts.

title: A path traversal vulnerability exists in the `extract_archive_to_dir` function within the `mlflow...
description: A path traversal vulnerability exists in the `extract_archive_to_dir` function within the `mlflow/pyfunc/dbconnect_artifact_cache.py` file of the mlflow/mlflow repository. This vulnerability, present in versions before v3.7.0, arises due to the lack of validation of tar member paths during extraction. An attacker with control over the tar.gz file can exploit this issue to overwrite arbitrary files or gain elevated privileges, potentially escaping the sandbox directory in multi-tenant or shared c

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The path traversal vulnerability in the `extract_archive_to_dir` function of the mlflow/mlflow repository allows attackers to overwrite arbitrary files or gain elevated privileges by exploiting improperly validated tar member paths. This could lead to data breaches, privilege escalation, or compromise of shared environments, especially in multi-tenant or cloud-based deployments.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to mlflow version 3.7.0 or later, where the vulnerability has been patched.
Implement input validation and sanitization for tar.gz files to ensure paths are restricted to the intended directory.
Restrict access to the vulnerable function and monitor for suspicious activity, especially in shared or multi-tenant environments.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-2370.

title: Hardcoded Password Vulnerability have been found in CENTUM. Affected products contain a hardcoded...
description:
Hardcoded Password Vulnerability have been found in CENTUM. Affected products contain a hardcoded password for the user account (PROG) used for CENTUM Authentication Mode within the system. Under the following conditions, there is a risk that an attacker could log in as the PROG user.

The default permission for the PROG users is S1 permission (equivalent to OFFUSER). Therefore, for properly permission-controlled targets of operation and monitoring, even if an attacker user in as the PROG user,

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The hardcoded password vulnerability in CENTUM could allow unauthorized access to the system using the PROG user account. However, the default permissions for this account are limited, reducing the potential impact. Properly permission-controlled systems are less likely to be affected.
recommended_actions: Immediately update affected systems to remove or disable the hardcoded password for the PROG user account.
Implement strong access control measures and monitor system logs for unauthorized login attempts.
Educate system administrators about the vulnerability and ensure proper permission settings are in place.

title: A security flaw has been discovered in Totolink A3300R 17.0.0cu.557_b20221024. This vulnerability...
description: A security flaw has been discovered in Totolink A3300R 17.0.0cu.557_b20221024. This vulnerability affects the function setSmartQosCfg of the file /cgi-bin/cstecgi.cgi of the component Parameter Handler. The manipulation of the argument qos_up_bw results in command injection. The attack can be executed remotely. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Totolink A3300R allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary commands via command injection, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data exfiltration, or complete system compromise. The public availability of the exploit increases the likelihood of attacks.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the Totolink A3300R firmware to the latest version if a patch is available. Contact the vendor for updates if necessary.
Implement network segmentation to isolate vulnerable devices from critical systems and sensitive data.
Monitor network traffic for unusual activity and apply intrusion detection/prevention systems to detect exploitation attempts.

title: Ghidra versions prior to 12.0.3 improperly process annotation directives embedded in...
description: Ghidra versions prior to 12.0.3 improperly process annotation directives embedded in automatically extracted binary data, resulting in arbitrary command execution when an analyst interacts with the UI. Specifically, the @execute annotation (which is intended for trusted, user-authored comments) is also parsed in comments generated during auto-analysis (such as CFStrings in Mach-O binaries). This allows a crafted binary to present seemingly benign clickable text which, when clicked, executes atta

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-4946.

title: Perl versions from 5.9.4 before 5.40.4-RC1, from 5.41.0 before 5.42.2-RC1, from 5.43.0 before 5...
description:
Perl versions from 5.9.4 before 5.40.4-RC1, from 5.41.0 before 5.42.2-RC1, from 5.43.0 before 5.43.9 contain a vulnerable version of Compress::Raw::Zlib.

Compress::Raw::Zlib is included in the Perl package as a dual-life core module, and is vulnerable to CVE-2026-3381 due to a vendored version of zlib which has several vulnerabilities, including CVE-2026-27171. The bundled Compress::Raw::Zlib was updated to version 2.221 in Perl blead commit c75ae9cc164205e1b6d6dbd57bd2c65c8593fe94.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-4176.

title: A Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulnerability exists in parisneo/lollms versions prior to 2...
description: A Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulnerability exists in parisneo/lollms versions prior to 2.2.0, specifically in the `/api/files/export-content` endpoint. The `_download_image_to_temp()` function in `backend/routers/files.py` fails to validate user-controlled URLs, allowing attackers to make arbitrary HTTP requests to internal services and cloud metadata endpoints. This vulnerability can lead to internal network access, cloud metadata access, information disclosure, port scanning, and poten

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The SSRF vulnerability in parisneo/lollms versions prior to 2.2.0 allows attackers to exploit the `/api/files/export-content` endpoint to perform unauthorized HTTP requests. This can lead to internal network access, exposure of cloud metadata, information disclosure, and potential lateral movement within the network. The vulnerability poses a significant risk to sensitive data and internal systems.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to parisneo/lollms version 2.2.0 or later to patch the SSRF vulnerability.
Implement strict input validation and URL whitelisting for user-controlled inputs in the `_download_image_to_temp()` function.
Monitor network traffic for unusual HTTP requests to internal services and cloud metadata endpoints, and apply appropriate firewall rules to block unauthorized access.

title: A critical security vulnerability in parisneo/lollms versions up to 2.2.0 allows any...
description: A critical security vulnerability in parisneo/lollms versions up to 2.2.0 allows any authenticated user to accept or reject friend requests belonging to other users. The `respond_request()` function in `backend/routers/friends.py` does not implement proper authorization checks, enabling Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) attacks. Specifically, the `/api/friends/requests/{friendship_id}` endpoint fails to verify whether the authenticated user is part of the friendship or the intended recipie

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated users to manipulate friend requests of other users without proper authorization checks. This can lead to unauthorized access, privacy violations, and potential abuse of user relationships, undermining trust in the platform.
recommended_actions: Implement proper authorization checks in the `respond_request()` function to ensure that only users involved in the friendship can modify its state.
Conduct a thorough code review of the authentication and authorization mechanisms across the application to identify and fix similar vulnerabilities.
Release a security patch for versions up to 2.2.0 and notify users to update to the latest secure version immediately.

title: A vulnerability in parisneo/lollms, up to and including version 2.2.0, allows unauthenticated...
description: A vulnerability in parisneo/lollms, up to and including version 2.2.0, allows unauthenticated users to upload and process files through the `/api/files/extract-text` endpoint. This endpoint does not enforce authentication, unlike other file-related endpoints, and lacks the `Depends(get_current_active_user)` dependency. This issue can lead to denial of service (DoS) through resource exhaustion, information disclosure, and violation of the application's documented security policies.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-0558.

description: In Sofia on Xiongmai DVR/NVR (AHB7008T-MH-V2 and NBD7024H-P) 4.03.R11 devices, root OS command injection can occur via shell metacharacters in the HostName value via an authenticated DVRIP protocol (TCP port 34567) request to the NetWork.NetCommon configuration handler, because system() is used.

title: OpenClaw has ACP CLI approval prompt ANSI escape sequence injection
description:
## Summary

ACP CLI approval prompt ANSI escape sequence injection

## Affected Packages / Versions

- Package: `openclaw`
- Affected versions: `>= 2026.2.13, <= 2026.3.24`
- First patched version: `2026.3.25`
- Latest published npm version at verification time: `2026.3.24`

## Details

ACP tool titles could previously carry ANSI control sequences into approval prompts and permission logs, letting untrusted tool metadata spoof terminal output. Commit `464e2c10a5edceb380d815adb6ff56e1a4c50f60` sa

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity.

title: OpenClaw: Telegram DM-Scoped Inline Button Callbacks Bypass DM Pairing and Mutate Session State
description:
## Summary

Telegram DM-Scoped Inline Button Callbacks Bypass DM Pairing and Mutate Session State

## Affected Packages / Versions

- Package: `openclaw`
- Affected versions: `<= 2026.3.24`
- First patched version: `2026.3.25`
- Latest published npm version at verification time: `2026.3.24`

## Details

Telegram callback queries from direct messages previously used weaker callback-only authorization and could mutate session state without satisfying normal DM pairing. Commit `269282ac69ab6030d5f3

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthorized manipulation of session states in Telegram direct messages by bypassing DM pairing through weaker callback-only authorization. This could lead to unauthorized actions, data integrity issues, or potential exploitation of user sessions.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to the patched version `2026.3.25` or later to mitigate the vulnerability.
Audit session state management and callback authorization mechanisms to ensure compliance with security best practices.
Monitor for unusual session activity and implement additional logging to detect potential exploitation attempts.

title: OpenClaw: Synology Chat Webhook Pre-Auth Rate-Limit Bypass Enables Brute-Force Guessing of Webhook Token
description:
## Summary

Synology Chat Webhook Pre-Auth Rate-Limit Bypass Enables Brute-Force Guessing of Weak Webhook Token

## Affected Packages / Versions

- Package: `openclaw`
- Affected versions: `<= 2026.3.24`
- First patched version: `2026.3.25`
- Latest published npm version at verification time: `2026.3.24`

## Details

Synology Chat webhook auth previously rejected invalid tokens without throttling repeated guesses, allowing brute-force attempts against weak webhook secrets. Commit `0b4d07337467f4

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity.

title: CVE-2025-10679 - The ReviewX – WooCommerce Product Reviews with Multi-Criteria, Reminder Emails, Google Reviews, Sche
description: The ReviewX – WooCommerce Product Reviews with Multi-Criteria, Reminder Emails, Google Reviews, Schema & More plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to arbitrary method calls in all versions up to, and including, 2.2.12. This is due to insufficient input validation in the bulkTenReviews function that allows user-controlled data to be passed directly to a variable function call mechanism. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to call arbitrary PHP class methods that take no inputs or have default values, potentially leading to information disclosure or remote code execution depending on available methods and server configuration.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2025-10679.

title: CVE-2026-4572 - A weakness has been identified in SourceCodester Sales and Inventory System 1.0. Affected by this is
description: A weakness has been identified in SourceCodester Sales and Inventory System 1.0. Affected by this issue is some unknown functionality of the file /view_product.php of the component HTTP POST Request Handler. Executing a manipulation of the argument searchtxt can lead to sql injection. The attack may be performed from remote. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows remote attackers to execute SQL injection attacks by exploiting the 'searchtxt' parameter in HTTP POST requests. This could lead to unauthorized data access, data manipulation, or complete database compromise, potentially exposing sensitive information.
recommended_actions: Immediately sanitize and validate all user inputs, especially the 'searchtxt' parameter, to prevent SQL injection.
Apply a web application firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious SQL queries targeting the vulnerable endpoint.
Check for and apply any available patches or updates from the SourceCodester development team to address this vulnerability.

title: CVE-2026-4606 - GV Edge Recording Manager (ERM) v2.3.1 improperly runs application components with SYSTEM-level priv
description:
GV Edge Recording Manager (ERM) v2.3.1 improperly runs application components with SYSTEM-level privileges, allowing any local user to gain full control of the operating system. 

During installation, ERM creates a Windows service that runs under the LocalSystem account. 

When the ERM application is launched, related processes are spawned under SYSTEM privileges rather than the security context of the logged-in user. 

Functions such as 'Import Data' open a Windows file dialog operating with SYSTEM permissions, enabling modification or deletion of protected system files and directories. 

Any ERM function invoking Windows file open/save dialogs exposes the same risk. 

This vulnerability allows local privilege escalation and may result in full system compromise.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-4606.

title: CVE-2026-2580 - The WP Maps – Store Locator,Google Maps,OpenStreetMap,Mapbox,Listing,Directory & Filters plugin for
description: The WP Maps – Store Locator,Google Maps,OpenStreetMap,Mapbox,Listing,Directory & Filters plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to time-based SQL Injection via the ‘orderby’ parameter in all versions up to, and including, 4.9.1 due to insufficient escaping on the user supplied parameter and lack of sufficient preparation on the existing SQL query.  This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to append additional SQL queries into already existing queries that can be used to extract sensitive information from the database.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-2580.
